example_prompt: Create a Flutterwave v4 orchestrated transfer inline (without pre-creating sender or recipient) to send a payout across borders in a single request.

reference.description: Optional merchant-supplied unique reference. Alphanumeric and hyphens, 6-42 chars. Reusing returns 409.

narration.description: Optional narration shown to the recipient where supported.

transfer_purpose.description: Purpose of the transfer. Required for bank_egp instructions; optional for other corridors.

payment_instruction.description: Discriminated union keyed on `type`. The destination_currency is fixed by `type` (e.g. bank_ngn => NGN). The shape of `recipient` and `sender` varies by corridor (bank_ngn_instruction, bank_usd_instruction, bank_gbp_instruction, bank_eur_instruction, bank_egp_instruction, bank_etb_instruction, bank_ghs_instruction, bank_mwk_instruction, bank_zar_instruction).

type.description: Discriminator that selects the corridor and fixes the destination currency.

destination_currency.description: Read-only. Inferred from `type` (e.g. NGN for bank_ngn).

applies_to.description: Whether `value` is denominated in the destination or source currency.

recipient.description: Corridor-specific recipient shape. For bank_ngn: `{ bank: { account_number, code } }`. For bank_usd: full name+phone+email+address+bank (account_number 5-17, account_type checking|savings, routing_number 6-9 digits, swift_code 8-11). For bank_gbp: name+phone+email+address+bank (account_number 8-35, account_type individual|corporate, name 2-60, sort_code 4-15). For bank_eur: name+phone+email+address+bank (account_number 8-35, name 2-60, swift_code 4-15). For bank_egp: bank (account_number 8-32, code), name, national_identification (type PASSPORT|DRIVERS_LICENSE|NATIONAL_ID, identifier, expiration_date), phone, address. For bank_etb: name + bank (account_number 7-24, code). For bank_ghs: name + bank (account_number 7-35, code, branch). For bank_mwk: name + bank (account_number 5-19, code, optional branch). For bank_zar: name+phone+email+address + bank (account_number 7-11, code).

sender.description: Corridor-specific sender shape (optional for purely local corridors). For bank_ngn / bank_etb / bank_ghs / bank_mwk / bank_zar: `{ name: { first, last } }`. For bank_usd / bank_gbp / bank_eur: name+email+address+phone. For bank_egp: name, national_identification, date_of_birth (YYYY-MM-DD), phone, email, address.

date_time.description: Scheduled disbursement date/time in 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' (24-hour) format.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (Africa/Cairo, Africa/Lagos, America/New_York, Asia/Dubai, Europe/London, UTC, ...).

callback_url.description: Webhook URL Flutterwave POSTs to on transfer status changes. Overrides the merchant default webhook URL.

meta.description: Arbitrary key/value metadata stored on the transfer.

response_schema.description: 201 Created. Standard Flutterwave envelope with the full orchestrated transfer object.

id.description: Transfer ID — use with /transfers/{id} for retrieval, update and retry.

recipient.description: Resolved recipient including the auto-created recipient.id.

sender.description: Resolved sender including the auto-created sender.id.

gotchas: Flutterwave v4 uses OAuth2 client credentials. POST client_id and client_secret as form-encoded body to https://idp.flutterwave.com/realms/flutterwave/protocol/openid-connect/token to obtain an access_token (valid 10 minutes), then use it as Bearer in the Authorization header. The FLUTTERWAVE_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS env var stores base64(client_id:client_secret).

POST /direct-transfers is the orchestrator variant: it accepts inline sender and recipient objects and creates those records on the fly, returning their ids in the response. Use it when you do not want to pre-call POST /transfers/senders and POST /transfers/recipients. For repeated payouts to the same beneficiary, switch to POST /transfers with stored sender_id/recipient_id.

payment_instruction.type is a discriminator that fixes the destination corridor (bank_ngn, bank_usd, bank_gbp, bank_eur, bank_egp, bank_etb, bank_ghs, bank_mwk, bank_zar). The required shape of recipient and sender changes per type — sending a bank_ngn recipient under bank_usd returns 400 with validation_errors.

Reference is optional but if supplied must be 6-42 alphanumeric+hyphen characters and unique. Always send X-Trace-Id (12-255 chars, required) and X-Idempotency-Key (12-255 chars, optional but strongly recommended) so retries are safe and the request is correlated in Flutterwave's logs.

amount.applies_to controls whether amount.value is denominated in destination_currency or source_currency — pick deliberately on cross-currency corridors, because Flutterwave will FX the other side using its quoted rate.
For bank_egp, transfer_purpose is required and national_identification (type/identifier/expiration_date) plus date_of_birth on the sender are mandatory. Skipping them returns 400.
Response status values are UPPERCASE (NEW, PENDING, FAILED, SUCCESSFUL, CANCELLED, INITIATED). Do not lowercase before comparison.
